channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Australian police uncover 3 tons of cocaine
item.description: The drug was found on June 19 in plastic tubs buried in bunkers hidden beneath three shipping containers. Two Sydney residents were arrested. They face potential sentences of life in prison.
item.content:encoded:
The drug was found on June 19 in plastic tubs buried in bunkers hidden beneath three shipping containers. Two Sydney residents were arrested. They face potential sentences of life in prison.

(Image credit: Australian Federal Police via AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: China hits back at U.S. sanctions restricting its exports to American defense firms
item.description: China on Monday announced sanctions on 10 American military-related companies in response to a recent U.S. move that bars some leading Chinese tech companies from defense contracts.
item.content:encoded:
China on Monday announced sanctions on 10 American military-related companies in response to a recent U.S. move that bars some leading Chinese tech companies from defense contracts.

(Image credit: Mark Schiefelbein)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Colombia Election: Right-wing Abelardo de la Espriella wins, initial count shows
item.description: Colombia wakes up to a sharp political turn to right as Abelardo de la Espriella's preliminary victory redraws the country's path on security, economy, and peace.
item.content:encoded:
Colombia wakes up to a sharp political turn to right as Abelardo de la Espriella's preliminary victory redraws the country's path on security, economy, and peace.

(Image credit: Rodrigo Abd)
item.dc:creator: John Otis

item.title: Leading Lebanese conservationist dies after Israeli airstrike on her home
item.description: Mona Khalil died Friday after an Israeli airstrike hit her beachside home two weeks ago. She's credited with creating a conservation movement in southern Lebanon to protect sea turtle nesting grounds.
item.content:encoded:
Mona Khalil died Friday after an Israeli airstrike hit her beachside home two weeks ago. She's credited with creating a conservation movement in southern Lebanon to protect sea turtle nesting grounds.

(Image credit: Jihad Seqlawi)
item.dc:creator: Jane Arraf

item.title: Colombia weighs peace talks against a tougher approach
item.description: Colombia's government is touting a rare peace deal with a rebel group. But the front-runner in today's presidential election says he'll abandon negotiations. NPR's John Otis reports.
item.content:encoded: Colombia's government is touting a rare peace deal with a rebel group. But the front-runner in today's presidential election says he'll abandon negotiations. NPR's John Otis reports.
item.dc:creator: John Otis

item.title: Hormuz dispute clouds Iran peace talks
item.description: At the Lake Lucerne summit, U.S. and Iranian officials are trying to keep a shaky peace effort alive after renewed violence and confusion over Hormuz. Former Ambassador Ryan Crocker talks about the need for strategic patience.
item.content:encoded: At the Lake Lucerne summit, U.S. and Iranian officials are trying to keep a shaky peace effort alive after renewed violence and confusion over Hormuz. Former Ambassador Ryan Crocker talks about the need for strategic patience.
item.dc:creator: Jason Fuller

item.title: Cambodian scam center humanitarian crisis
item.description: Cambodia is cracking down on cyber scam compounds linked to human trafficking and thousands of workers have been freed. But many say they're now stranded, with nowhere to stay and no way to get back to their families.
item.content:encoded: Cambodia is cracking down on cyber scam compounds linked to human trafficking and thousands of workers have been freed. But many say they're now stranded, with nowhere to stay and no way to get back to their families.
item.dc:creator: Shibani Mahtani

item.title: Trump threatens to 'hit Iran very hard again' while Vance is in Switzerland for talks
item.description: President Trump has threatened further attacks on Iran while Vice President Vance attended talks with Iranian officials in Switzerland on Sunday.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump has threatened further attacks on Iran while Vice President Vance attended talks with Iranian officials in Switzerland on Sunday.

(Image credit: Nathan Howard)
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster

item.title: Ukrainian attacks prompt Russian-held Crimea to halt civilian gasoline sales
item.description: Officials in Russia-occupied Crimea suspended civilian gasoline sales Sunday as Ukraine ramped up attacks on fuel supplies on the Black Sea peninsula.
item.content:encoded:
Officials in Russia-occupied Crimea suspended civilian gasoline sales Sunday as Ukraine ramped up attacks on fuel supplies on the Black Sea peninsula.

(Image credit: Andriy Andriyenko/Ukrainian 65 Mechanized brigade)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: What could make a peace deal with Iran viable? One expert weighs in
item.description: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe asks Ali Vaez, director of the Iran Project at the International Crisis Group, about the U.S.-Iran memorandum of understanding and the priorities for a future peace deal.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe asks Ali Vaez, director of the Iran Project at the International Crisis Group, about the U.S.-Iran memorandum of understanding and the priorities for a future peace deal.
item.dc:creator: Ayesha Rascoe
